908	\section{The genus-$0$ base objects and the $\mathbb G_m$-scaling action (the primary route)}
909	
910	\begin{definition}
911	[Concrete genus-$0$ base objects: $\mathbb P^1$, $\mathbb G_a$, $\mathbb G_m$ over $\bar k$]
912	  \label{def:genus0_base_objects}
913	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveLineBar}
914	  % Archon-original Lean encoding of standard objects; the mathematics is textbook (Hartshorne
915	  % I.2--3 for the projective line; the group laws on $\mathbb A^1$ are elementary). No external
916	  % % SOURCE QUOTE is needed for the Lean encoding itself.
917	  Work over $\Spec \bar k$ with $\bar k$ algebraically closed. The primary $\mathbb G_m$-scaling
918	  route (and its $\mathbb G_a$-additive alternative) needs only three concrete objects of
919	  $\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec \bar k)$:
920	  \begin{itemize}
921	    \item $\mathbb P^1 = \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$, the projective line --- a smooth proper
922	      geometrically irreducible curve of genus $0$ (Mathlib: projective space, or $\mathrm{Proj}$
923	      of the graded polynomial ring; \lean name \texttt{ProjectiveLineBar} [expected]). It carries
924	      the distinguished $\bar k$-points $0$, $1$, $\infty$ and the two standard affine charts
925	      $\mathbb A^1 = \mathbb P^1 \setminus \{\infty\}$ (coordinate $x$) and
926	      $\mathbb P^1 \setminus \{0\}$ (coordinate $u = 1/x$).
927	    \item $\mathbb G_a = \mathbb A^1_{\bar k}$, the affine line as the \emph{additive} group object
928	      (Mathlib: \texttt{AffineSpace}\,$\mathbb A(1; \Spec \bar k)$ with the $\mathtt{GrpObj}$
929	      additive structure $(x, y) \mapsto x + y$, identity $0$; \lean name \texttt{Ga} [expected]).
930	      As a scheme $\mathbb G_a = \mathbb A^1 = \mathbb P^1 \setminus \{\infty\}$, and the open
931	      immersion $\mathbb G_a \hookrightarrow \mathbb P^1$ has dense (cofinite) image.
932	    \item $\mathbb G_m = \mathbb A^1 \setminus \{0\} = \mathbb P^1 \setminus \{0, \infty\}$, the
933	      \emph{multiplicative} group object (group law $(x, y) \mapsto xy$, identity $1$; \lean name
934	      \texttt{Gm} [expected]). Its open immersion into $\mathbb P^1$ also has dense image.
935	  \end{itemize}
936	  All three are of finite type over $\bar k$; $\mathbb P^1$ is proper, while $\mathbb G_a$ and
937	  $\mathbb G_m$ are affine (hence not complete). These are the only ``genus-$0$'' geometric inputs
938	  the base case needs.
939	\end{definition}
940	
941	\begin{definition}
942	[The $\mathbb G_m$-scaling action $\sigma_\times \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to \mathbb P^1$ (primary), and the $\mathbb G_a$-translation companion $\sigma$]
943	  \label{def:gaTranslationP1}
944	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gmScalingP1}
945	  \uses{def:genus0_base_objects}
946	  % Archon-original Lean encoding; the chart computations below are elementary and characteristic-free.
947	  % No external % SOURCE QUOTE for the encoding. The mathematical fact -- $\mathbb G_m$ acts on
948	  % $\mathbb P^1$ by scalings fixing $0$ and $\infty$, $\mathbb G_a$ by translations fixing $\infty$
949	  % -- is standard (the Borel subgroup of $\mathrm{PGL}_2$ acting on $\mathbb P^1$).
950	  % NOTE (iter-164): the load-bearing action for the PRIMARY genus-0 route is now the MULTIPLICATIVE
951	  % scaling action $\sigma_\times$ (\lean name gmScalingP1 [expected]); the $\mathbb G_a$-translation
952	  % $\sigma$ (\lean name gaTranslationP1 [expected]) is retained only for the demoted additive
953	  % alternative. Both expected Lean targets are listed in the writer report for scaffolding.
954	  \textbf{The scaling action (primary).} The \emph{multiplicative scaling action}
955	  \[ \sigma_\times \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to \mathbb P^1, \qquad
956	     \text{``}\sigma_\times(x, \lambda) = \lambda x\text{''} \]
957	  (\lean name \texttt{gmScalingP1} [expected]) is a \emph{genuine total} scheme morphism on all of
958	  $\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m$ --- no indeterminacy, characteristic-free (M\"obius scaling
959	  fixing both $0$ and $\infty$) --- defined chartwise:
960	  \begin{itemize}
961	    \item On $\mathbb A^1 \times \mathbb G_m$ (target chart $x$): $(x, \lambda) \mapsto \lambda x$,
962	      a polynomial map, regular everywhere.
963	    \item Near $\infty$ (target chart $u = 1/x$): the target coordinate $1/(\lambda x)$ rewrites as
964	      $u/\lambda$, regular everywhere on this chart because $\lambda \in \mathbb G_m$ is invertible.
965	  \end{itemize}
966	  The two target charts cover the image and overlap consistently, so $\sigma_\times$ is defined on
967	  the whole surface. It is a left action of the group $\mathbb G_m$ on $\mathbb P^1$:
968	  $\sigma_\times(x, 1) = x$ and $\sigma_\times(\sigma_\times(x, \lambda), \mu) = \sigma_\times(x,
969	  \lambda\mu)$. Crucially it has \emph{two fixed points}, $\sigma_\times(0, \lambda) = 0$ and
970	  $\sigma_\times(\infty, \lambda) = \infty$ for all $\lambda$; the fixed point $0$ is what makes the
971	  $W$-axis of the additive decomposition collapse in the primary base-case proof
972	  (\cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}, \cref{rmk:base_case_fourth_route}).
973	
974	  \medskip
975	  \noindent\textbf{Companion: the $\mathbb G_a$-translation action (demoted route only).} For the
976	  Milne-faithful additive alternative we also record the \emph{translation action}
977	  \[ \sigma \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_a \to \mathbb P^1, \qquad
978	     \text{``}\sigma(x, y) = x + y\text{''} \]
979	  (\lean name \texttt{gaTranslationP1} [expected]), likewise a \emph{total} scheme morphism on all
980	  of $\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_a$, defined chartwise: on $\mathbb A^1 \times \mathbb G_a$
981	  (chart $x$) it is the polynomial map $(x, y) \mapsto x + y$; near $\infty$ ($u = 1/x$) the
982	  coordinate $1/(x + y)$ rewrites as $u/(1 + u y)$, regular at $u = 0$ (value $0$ there --- $\sigma$
983	  fixes $\infty$ for every $y$), while the locus $x + y = 0$ is covered by the chart $x$ on which it
984	  is the finite value $0$. It is a left action of $\mathbb G_a$ on $\mathbb P^1$ fixing $\infty$:
985	  $\sigma(x, 0) = x$ and $\sigma(\sigma(x, y), y') = \sigma(x, y + y')$. This companion underlies
986	  only the alternative proof of \cref{lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial} and the demoted $\mathbb
987	  G_a$-additive close of the base case (\cref{lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial}); it is \emph{not} on the
988	  genus-$0$ critical path.
989	\end{definition}
990	
991	\begin{lemma}
992	[$\mathrm{Hom}(\mathbb G_a, A) = 0$: a homomorphism from the additive group into an abelian variety is trivial]
993	  \label{lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial}
994	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.hom_Ga_to_av_trivial}
995	  \uses{def:genus0_base_objects, def:gaTranslationP1, lem:hom_additivity_over_product, lem:eq_comp_of_isAffine_of_properIntegral}
996	  % SOURCE: [Milne, Abelian Varieties], Proposition 3.9, \S I.3, doc p.~19--20 (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF pages 25--26)
997	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "PROPOSITION 3.9. Every rational map A¹ ⇢ A or P¹ ⇢ A is constant."
998	  % NOTE (iter-164): the verbatim Milne Prop 3.9 statement+proof quotes are the prior-session-verified
999	  % copies also carried on \cref{lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial} below. This host lacks a PDF renderer
1000	  % (pdftoppm) so the quotes were NOT re-rendered from references/abelian-varieties.pdf this session;
1001	  % they are reproduced from the verified in-tree copy. See the writer report.
1002	  \textit{Source: Milne, Abelian Varieties, Proposition~3.9.}
1003	
1004	  \textbf{Off the genus-$0$ critical path (iter-164).} This $\mathrm{Hom}(\mathbb G_a, A) = 0$
1005	  lemma is \emph{superseded by the $\mathbb G_m$-scaling shortcut}
1006	  (\cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}, \cref{rmk:base_case_fourth_route}), which closes the
1007	  base case without it. It is retained only as the classical Milne Proposition~3.9 ($\mathbb
1008	  G_a/\mathbb G_m$ incompatibility) argument feeding the alternative route
1009	  \cref{lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial}; no block on the genus-$0$ critical path depends on it.
1010	
1011	  Let $A$ be an abelian variety over the algebraically closed field $\bar k$, and let
1012	  $\varphi \colon \mathbb G_a \to A$ be a homomorphism of group objects (so $\varphi(0) = 0_A$ and
1013	  $\varphi(x + y) = \varphi(x) + \varphi(y)$). Then $\varphi$ is the trivial homomorphism: it
1014	  factors through the terminal object as the constant morphism at the identity,
1015	  $\varphi = \mathtt{toUnit}\,\mathbb G_a \fatsemi \eta_A$.
1016	
1017	  \medskip
1018	  \noindent\textbf{Equivalent completeness phrasing.} Equivalently: a homomorphism from an
1019	  \emph{affine connected} group object into a \emph{complete} group object is trivial. That is the
1020	  abstract content; the proof below realises it characteristic-free.
1021	\end{lemma}
1022	
1023	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "PROOF. According to (3.2), α extends to a regular map on the whole of A¹.
1024	% After composing α with a translation, we may suppose that α(0)=0. Then α is a homomorphism,
1025	% α(x+y)=α(x)+α(y); all x,y ∈ A¹(k^al)=k^al. But A¹−{0} is also a group variety, and similarly,
1026	% α(xy)=α(x)+α(y)+c; all x,y ∈ A¹(k^al)=k^al. This is absurd, unless α is constant." [Milne,
1027	% Prop 3.9, \S I.3, doc p.~19--20.]
1028	\begin{proof}
1029	  \uses{lem:hom_additivity_over_product, lem:eq_comp_of_isAffine_of_properIntegral}
1030	  \textit{Source: Milne, Abelian Varieties, Proposition~3.9.}
1031	  Write $A$ additively (an abelian variety is commutative). We give the conceptual
1032	  \emph{completeness} proof as the abstract argument, then record Milne's elementary $\mathbb
1033	  G_a/\mathbb G_m$ incompatibility, which applies whenever $\varphi$ extends to a morphism on
1034	  $\mathbb P^1$ (as it does in the genus-$0$ application).
1035	
1036	  \textbf{Completeness proof.} The image $\varphi(\mathbb G_a)$ is a connected subgroup of $A$
1037	  (image of the connected $\mathbb G_a$ under a homomorphism). As the image of a homomorphism of
1038	  algebraic groups it is a \emph{closed} subgroup of $A$, hence \emph{complete} (closed in the
1039	  complete $A$); as a homomorphic image of the \emph{affine} $\mathbb G_a$ it is moreover
1040	  \emph{affine}. A connected variety that is simultaneously complete and affine over $\bar k$ is a
1041	  single point: its ring of global regular functions is a field finite over $\bar k$, hence equal
1042	  to $\bar k$ (the $\Gamma = \bar k$ collapse already packaged for proper integral sources in
1043	  \cref{lem:eq_comp_of_isAffine_of_properIntegral}). Therefore $\varphi(\mathbb G_a)$ is one point;
1044	  being a homomorphism, $\varphi$ sends $0$ there, so the point is $0_A$ and
1045	  $\varphi = \mathtt{toUnit}\,\mathbb G_a \fatsemi \eta_A$.
1046	
1047	  \medskip
1048	  \noindent\textbf{Milne's elementary $\mathbb G_a/\mathbb G_m$ incompatibility (when $\varphi$
1049	  extends to $\mathbb P^1$).} Suppose $\varphi = f|_{\mathbb G_a}$ for a morphism
1050	  $f \colon \mathbb P^1 \to A$ (the genus-$0$ situation). Set $g := f - f(1)$, so $g(1) = 0_A$, and
1051	  form $h_\times := \sigma_\times \fatsemi g \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to A$ with the
1052	  scaling companion $\sigma_\times$ of \cref{def:gaTranslationP1}. Applying the additive
1053	  decomposition \cref{lem:hom_additivity_over_product} with $V = \mathbb P^1$ (complete),
1054	  $W = \mathbb G_m$, base points $1 \in \mathbb P^1$ and $1 \in \mathbb G_m$ (so
1055	  $h_\times(1, 1) = g(1) = 0_A$), the axis restrictions are $x \mapsto g(x)$ and
1056	  $\lambda \mapsto g(\lambda)$, whence $g(\lambda x) = g(x) + g(\lambda)$ on $\bar k^\times$;
1057	  re-expanding $g$ gives the multiplicative relation
1058	  $\varphi(xy) = \varphi(x) + \varphi(y) + c$ with $c = -\varphi(1)$. Now $\varphi$ is at once
1059	  additive, $\varphi(x + y) = \varphi(x) + \varphi(y)$, and ``multiplicative-additive'',
1060	  $\varphi(xy) = \varphi(x) + \varphi(y) + c$. Computing $\varphi((x + x')\,y)$ in two ways for
1061	  $x, x' \in \bar k$, $y \in \bar k^\times$,
1062	  \[ \varphi((x+x')y) = \varphi(xy) + \varphi(x'y) = \varphi(x) + \varphi(x') + 2\varphi(y) + 2c, \]
1063	  while also $\varphi((x+x')y) = \varphi(x+x') + \varphi(y) + c = \varphi(x) + \varphi(x') +
1064	  \varphi(y) + c$; equating gives $\varphi(y) = -c = \varphi(1)$ for every $y \in \bar k^\times$.
1065	  Hence $\varphi$ is constant on the dense $\mathbb G_m \subset \mathbb G_a$, so constant on
1066	  $\mathbb G_a$ (density, $A$ separated), with value $\varphi(0) = 0_A$. This is Milne's argument
1067	  verbatim and uses only the proven \cref{lem:hom_additivity_over_product} plus the scaling action,
1068	  avoiding the ``image is a closed subgroup'' input of the completeness proof. \qedhere
1069	\end{proof}
1070	
1071	\begin{remark}
1072	  \label{rmk:base_case_fourth_route}
1073	  \textbf{The primary route is the $\mathbb G_m$-scaling shortcut; why no Theorem~3.2 and no
1074	  $\mathrm{Hom}(\mathbb G_a, A) = 0$ are needed.} The genus-$0$ base case ``$f \colon \mathbb P^1
1075	  \to A$ is constant'' is closed \emph{directly} by the proven additive decomposition
1076	  \cref{lem:hom_additivity_over_product} composed with a total $\mathbb G$-action on $\mathbb P^1$,
1077	  with \emph{no} appeal to Milne's Theorem~3.2 / Lemma~3.3 (rational-map extension), the theorem of
1078	  the cube, Auslander--Buchsbaum, or differentials. The reason the earlier draft thought
1079	  Theorem~3.2 was needed --- that the additive defect $\psi(x,y) = f(x+y) - f(x) - f(y)$ lives only
1080	  on the affine $\mathbb G_a \times \mathbb G_a$ and must be extended over $\mathbb P^1 \times
1081	  \mathbb P^1$ --- evaporates once one composes with a \emph{total} action morphism of
1082	  \cref{def:gaTranslationP1}: the composite is already a total morphism out of $\mathbb P^1 \times
1083	  \mathbb G$, and the complete factor is $\mathbb P^1$, exactly what
1084	  \cref{lem:hom_additivity_over_product} (whose Lean signature needs only the first factor
1085	  complete) consumes.
1086	
1087	  \medskip
1088	  \noindent\textbf{Primary route --- the scaling shortcut (strategy decision of iter-164).} The
1089	  \emph{multiplicative} scaling action $\sigma_\times \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to
1090	  \mathbb P^1$, $(x, \lambda) \mapsto \lambda x$ (\cref{def:gaTranslationP1}), closes the base case
1091	  in one shot, \emph{without} the additive homomorphism step or \cref{lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial} at
1092	  all. Normalise $f(0) = 0_A$ (translate; here $0$ is a scaling fixed point). Form $h_\times :=
1093	  \sigma_\times \fatsemi f$ and apply \cref{lem:hom_additivity_over_product} with $V = \mathbb P^1$,
1094	  $W = \mathbb G_m$, base points $0 \in \mathbb P^1$ and $1 \in \mathbb G_m$ (so $h_\times(0, 1) =
1095	  f(0) = 0_A$). Because $0$ is a \emph{fixed point} of scaling, the $W$-axis restriction collapses:
1096	  $\lambda \mapsto h_\times(0, \lambda) = f(\lambda \cdot 0) = f(0) = 0_A$. The decomposition
1097	  therefore reads $h_\times = p \fatsemi f$ with the second summand the group identity, i.e.
1098	  \[ \sigma_\times \fatsemi f = \mathrm{pr}_1 \fatsemi f \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to A,
1099	     \qquad\text{``}f(\lambda x) = f(x)\text{''}. \]
1100	  Precomposing with $\lambda \mapsto (1, \lambda)$ gives $f|_{\mathbb G_m} = \text{const}\,f(1)$;
1101	  since $\mathbb G_m$ is dense in $\mathbb P^1$ and $A$ is separated, $f$ is constant on $\mathbb
1102	  P^1$ (the dominant-source/separated-target handle \cref{thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen}, i.e.\ the
1103	  $\mathtt{ext\_of\_isDominant}$ family already used by \texttt{rigidity\_core}). This uses
1104	  \emph{only} the proven \cref{lem:hom_additivity_over_product}, the total morphism $\sigma_\times$,
1105	  and density --- no $\mathbb G_a$ additivity, no \cref{lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial}, and in
1106	  particular \emph{no} ``image of a group homomorphism is a closed subgroup'' input (a deep Mathlib
1107	  gap). It is the least-Mathlib-blocked realisation of the base case and is the route the live
1108	  consumer \cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant} formalises.
1109	
1110	  \medskip
1111	  \noindent\textbf{Retained alternative --- the $\mathbb G_a$-additive route.} The Milne-faithful
1112	  argument routes through the translation action $\sigma \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_a \to
1113	  \mathbb P^1$: $\sigma \fatsemi f$ is total, the additive decomposition makes $f|_{\mathbb G_a}$ a
1114	  homomorphism $\mathbb G_a \to A$ (\cref{lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial}), and a homomorphism from the
1115	  additive group into an abelian variety is trivial (\cref{lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial}, Milne's
1116	  $\mathbb G_a/\mathbb G_m$ incompatibility, Proposition~3.9). This reaches the same conclusion but
1117	  needs the triviality lemma; it is retained for fidelity to Milne and is \emph{off} the genus-$0$
1118	  critical path.
1119	\end{remark}
1120	
1121	\begin{lemma}
1122	[A pointed $\mathbb P^1 \to A$ is constant (Milne Prop 3.9, via the 4th route)]
1123	  \label{lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial}
1124	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.morphism_Ga_to_av_const}
1125	  \uses{thm:rigidity_lemma, lem:hom_additivity_over_product, def:gaTranslationP1, lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial}
1126	  % SOURCE: [Milne, Abelian Varieties], Proposition 3.9, \S I.3, doc p.~19--20 (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF pages 25--26)
1127	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "PROPOSITION 3.9. Every rational map A¹ ⇢ A or P¹ ⇢ A is constant."
1128	  \textit{Source: Milne, Abelian Varieties, Proposition~3.9.}
1129	
1130	  \textbf{Off the genus-$0$ critical path (iter-164): retained alternative route.} This
1131	  $\mathbb G_a$-additive close of the base case is \emph{superseded by the $\mathbb G_m$-scaling
1132	  shortcut} now installed as the proof of \cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant} (see
1133	  \cref{rmk:base_case_fourth_route}). It is retained as the classical Milne Proposition~3.9
1134	  argument (faithful to the $\mathbb G_a \to A$ homomorphism / $\mathrm{Hom}(\mathbb G_a, A) = 0$
1135	  presentation), but the live consumer \cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant} no longer depends on
1136	  it; it is provided here only for completeness.
1137	
1138	  Let $A$ be an abelian variety over $\bar k$ and $f \colon \mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to A$ a
1139	  morphism with $f(0) = 0_A$ (the additive-identity $\bar k$-point of $\mathbb G_a = \mathbb A^1
1140	  \subset \mathbb P^1$; any base point reduces to this by a normalising translation in $A$).
1141	  Then $f$ is constant. Concretely: the translation action $\sigma$ (\cref{def:gaTranslationP1})
1142	  makes $f|_{\mathbb G_a}$ an additive-group homomorphism $\mathbb G_a \to A$, and a homomorphism
1143	  $\mathbb G_a \to A$ is trivial (\cref{lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial}), so $f$ collapses to the
1144	  constant $0_A$.
1145	\end{lemma}
1146	
1147	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "PROOF. According to (3.2), α extends to a regular map on the whole of A¹.
1148	% After composing α with a translation, we may suppose that α(0)=0. Then α is a homomorphism,
1149	% α(x+y)=α(x)+α(y); all x,y ∈ A¹(k^al)=k^al. But A¹−{0} is also a group variety, and similarly,
1150	% α(xy)=α(x)+α(y)+c; all x,y ∈ A¹(k^al)=k^al. This is absurd, unless α is constant." [Milne,
1151	% Prop 3.9, \S I.3, doc p.~19--20.]
1152	\begin{proof}
1153	  \uses{thm:rigidity_lemma, lem:hom_additivity_over_product, def:gaTranslationP1, lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial}
1154	  \textit{Source: Milne, Abelian Varieties, Proposition~3.9.}
1155	  Normalise $f(0) = 0_A$ by a translation in $A$ (un-translate at the end). The proof is the
1156	  \emph{4th route} (\cref{rmk:base_case_fourth_route}): the additive defect map is never built on
1157	  the affine surface and never extended; instead we precompose $f$ with the \emph{total}
1158	  translation action $\sigma$, and the complete factor is $\mathbb P^1$ itself, so the proven
1159	  additive decomposition applies directly. Milne's Theorem~3.2
1160	  (\cref{lem:rational_map_to_av_extends}) is \emph{not} used.
1161	
1162	  \textbf{$f|_{\mathbb G_a}$ is an additive homomorphism, via the total action $\sigma$.} Form
1163	  \[ h := \sigma \fatsemi f \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_a \to A, \qquad
1164	     \text{``}h(x, y) = f(x + y)\text{''}, \]
1165	  using the translation action $\sigma$ of \cref{def:gaTranslationP1}; this is a \emph{total}
1166	  morphism (no rational-map extension needed). Apply the additive decomposition
1167	  \cref{lem:hom_additivity_over_product} (Milne Cor~1.5) --- whose Lean signature requires only the
1168	  \emph{first} factor complete --- with $V = \mathbb P^1$ (proper), $W = \mathbb G_a$, and base
1169	  points $0 \in \mathbb P^1$, $0 \in \mathbb G_a$, noting $h(0, 0) = f(0) = 0_A$. The axis
1170	  restrictions are $x \mapsto h(x, 0) = f(x)$ and $y \mapsto h(0, y) = f(y)$, so the decomposition
1171	  $h = (p \fatsemi f) + (q \fatsemi (f|_{\mathbb G_a}))$ reads, on $\bar k$-points,
1172	  \[ f(x + y) = f(x) + f(y), \qquad x \in \mathbb P^1(\bar k),\ y \in \mathbb G_a(\bar k) = \bar k. \]
1173	  Restricting $x$ to $\mathbb G_a$ shows $f|_{\mathbb G_a} \colon \mathbb G_a \to A$ is a
1174	  homomorphism of group objects (it sends $0 \mapsto 0_A$ and respects addition).
1175	
1176	  \textbf{A homomorphism $\mathbb G_a \to A$ is trivial.} By \cref{lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial}
1177	  (the $\mathrm{Hom}(\mathbb G_a, A) = 0$ lemma, the content of Milne's $\mathbb G_a/\mathbb G_m$
1178	  incompatibility), $f|_{\mathbb G_a}$ is the constant morphism at $0_A$. Hence $f$ is constant on
1179	  the dense open $\mathbb G_a \subset \mathbb P^1$; since $\mathbb G_a$ is dense and $A$ is
1180	  separated, $f$ is constant on all of $\mathbb P^1$, with value $f(0) = 0_A$ (i.e.\ the original
1181	  base value before the normalising translation). \qedhere
1182	
1183	  \medskip
1184	  \noindent\emph{Note: this route is the demoted alternative.} The primary base-case proof is the
1185	  $\mathbb G_m$-scaling shortcut now installed directly as the proof of
1186	  \cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant} (\cref{rmk:base_case_fourth_route}): the multiplicative
1187	  scaling action $\sigma_\times$ of \cref{def:gaTranslationP1} gives $\sigma_\times \fatsemi f =
1188	  \mathrm{pr}_1 \fatsemi f$ (the $W$-axis collapses because $0$ is a scaling fixed point), so
1189	  $f|_{\mathbb G_m}$ is constant and $f$ is constant by density --- bypassing both the $\mathbb
1190	  G_a$-additivity step and \cref{lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial}, and avoiding the ``image is a closed
1191	  subgroup'' Mathlib gap. The present $\mathbb G_a$-additive lemma is retained only for fidelity to
1192	  Milne; it is not on the genus-$0$ critical path.
1193	\end{proof}
1194	
1195	\section{Morphisms from $\mathbb P^1$ to an abelian variety are constant}
1196	
1197	\begin{proposition}
1198	
1199	\leanok

1199	\leanok
1200	[A morphism $\mathbb P^1 \to A$ is constant]
1201	  \label{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}
1202	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const}
1203	  \uses{lem:hom_additivity_over_product, def:gaTranslationP1, thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen}
1204	  % SOURCE: [Milne, Abelian Varieties], Proposition 3.10 (specialised to a single $\mathbb P^1$ factor), \S I.3, doc p.~20 (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF page 26)
1205	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "PROPOSITION 3.10. Every rational map $\alpha \colon V \dashrightarrow A$
1206	  % from a unirational variety to an abelian variety is constant."
1207	  % SOURCE QUOTE (unirational, same page): "A variety $V$ over an algebraically closed field
1208	  % is said to be unirational if there is a dominating rational map $\mathbb A^n
1209	  % \dashrightarrow V$ with $n = \dim V$; equivalently, if $k(V)$ can be embedded into
1210	  % $k(X_1, \ldots, X_n)$ (pure transcendental extension of $k$). A variety $V$ over an
1211	  % arbitrary field $k$ is said to be unirational if $V_{k^{al}}$ is unirational."
1212	  \textit{Source: Milne, Abelian Varieties, Proposition~3.10 (case $V = \mathbb P^1$).}
1213	  Let $\bar k$ be algebraically closed and $A$ an abelian variety over $\bar k$. Then every
1214	  morphism $f \colon \mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to A$ is constant: $f(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k})$ is a
1215	  single $\bar k$-point.
1216	\end{proposition}
1217	
1218	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "PROOF. We may suppose that $k$ is algebraically closed. By assumption
1219	% there is a rational map $\mathbb A^n \dashrightarrow V$ with dense image, and the composite
1220	% of this with $\alpha$ extends to a morphism $\beta \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \cdots \times
1221	% \mathbb P^1 \to A$. An induction argument, starting from Corollary 1.5, shows that there are
1222	% regular maps $\beta_i \colon \mathbb P^1 \to A$ such that $\beta(x_1, \ldots, x_d) = \sum
1223	% \beta_i(x_i)$, and the lemma shows that each $\beta_i$ is constant."
1224	\begin{proof}
1225	  \uses{lem:hom_additivity_over_product, def:gaTranslationP1, thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen}
1226	  This is the single-factor ($d = 1$) instance of Milne's Proposition~3.10, where the
1227	  unirational variety is $\mathbb P^1$ itself. We give the project's \textbf{primary} proof, the
1228	  \emph{$\mathbb G_m$-scaling shortcut}: it proves constancy \emph{in one shot} from the proven
1229	  additive decomposition (\cref{lem:hom_additivity_over_product}) and a scaling fixed point,
1230	  without the intermediate ``$f|_{\mathbb G_a}$ additive'' step and \emph{without} the
1231	  $\mathrm{Hom}(\mathbb G_a, A) = 0$ triviality lemma. The theorem of the cube is \emph{not} used
1232	  (\cref{rmk:cube_not_needed}), and the map $f$ is already a morphism out of the complete
1233	  nonsingular $\mathbb P^1$, so Milne's Theorem~3.2 (\cref{lem:rational_map_to_av_extends}) is
1234	  \emph{not} invoked either.
1235	
1236	  \textbf{Reduction to a base point.} Choose the scaling fixed point $0 \in \mathbb G_a \subset
1237	  \mathbb P^1$ (one of the two fixed points of $\sigma_\times$). Translating in the group $A$ by
1238	  $-f(0)$, we may assume $f(0) = 0_A$; un-translating at the end restores the original value.
1239	
1240	  \textbf{The scaling shortcut.} Form the \emph{total} morphism
1241	  \[ h := \sigma_\times \fatsemi f \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to A, \qquad
1242	     \text{``}h(x, \lambda) = f(\lambda x)\text{''}, \]
1243	  using the multiplicative scaling action $\sigma_\times$ of \cref{def:gaTranslationP1} (a genuine
1244	  total morphism --- no rational-map extension needed). Apply the additive decomposition
1245	  \cref{lem:hom_additivity_over_product} (Milne Cor~1.5) --- whose Lean signature requires only the
1246	  \emph{first} factor complete --- with $V = \mathbb P^1$ (proper), $W = \mathbb G_m$, and base
1247	  points $0 \in \mathbb P^1$, $1 \in \mathbb G_m$; here $h(0, 1) = f(1 \cdot 0) = f(0) = 0_A$, as
1248	  required. The decomposition writes $h = (p \fatsemi f_0) + (q \fatsemi g)$ with $f_0$ the
1249	  $V$-axis restriction $x \mapsto h(x, 1) = f(1 \cdot x) = f(x)$ (so $f_0 = f$) and $g$ the
1250	  $W$-axis restriction $\lambda \mapsto h(0, \lambda) = f(\lambda \cdot 0) = f(0) = 0_A$.
1251	
1252	  \textbf{The $W$-axis collapses (the load-bearing scaling fixed point).} Because $0$ is a
1253	  \emph{fixed point} of scaling, $\sigma_\times(0, \lambda) = 0$ for all $\lambda$, so the $W$-axis
1254	  restriction $g$ is the \emph{constant} morphism at $0_A$ --- the identity of the hom-group
1255	  $\mathrm{Hom}(\mathbb G_m, A)$. The corresponding summand $q \fatsemi g$ is therefore the group
1256	  identity, and the decomposition collapses to
1257	  \[ h = p \fatsemi f, \qquad\text{i.e.}\qquad
1258	     \sigma_\times \fatsemi f = \mathrm{pr}_1 \fatsemi f, \qquad
1259	     \text{``}f(\lambda x) = f(x)\text{''} \quad (x \in \mathbb P^1,\ \lambda \in \mathbb G_m). \]
1260	
1261	  \textbf{Constancy via density.} Specialise $x = 1$ (precompose with $\lambda \mapsto (1,
1262	  \lambda)$): $f(\lambda) = f(1)$ for all $\lambda \in \mathbb G_m$, i.e.\ $f|_{\mathbb G_m}$ is
1263	  the constant morphism at $f(1)$. Since $\mathbb G_m$ is dense in the irreducible $\mathbb P^1$
1264	  and $A$ is separated, the two morphisms $f$ and $\mathrm{const}_{f(1)}$, agreeing on the
1265	  non-empty open $\mathbb G_m$, agree everywhere by the dominant-source/separated-target rigidity
1266	  handle \cref{thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen} (the same \texttt{ext\_of\_isDominant} family
1267	  \texttt{rigidity\_core} uses). Hence $f$ is constant, with value $f(1)$; in the normalised
1268	  picture $f(0) = 0_A$, so the constant is $0_A$ (the original $f(0)$ before translation).
1269	
1270	  \medskip
1271	  \noindent\emph{Multi-factor remark.} For the general unirational statement of Proposition~3.10,
1272	  one writes $\beta(x_1, \ldots, x_d) = \sum_i \beta_i(x_i)$ via the additive decomposition
1273	  (\cref{lem:hom_additivity_over_product}) and applies this base case to each $\beta_i \colon
1274	  \mathbb P^1 \to A$; we need only the $d = 1$ case here. The Milne-faithful $\mathbb
1275	  G_a$-additive route (\cref{lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial}, via $\mathrm{Hom}(\mathbb G_a, A) = 0$,
1276	  \cref{lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial}) reaches the same conclusion and is retained as an alternative,
1277	  but is \emph{not} on this critical path.
1278	\end{proof}
1279	
1280	\section{A genus-$0$ curve over $\bar k$ is isomorphic to $\mathbb P^1$}
1281	
1282	\begin{proposition}
1283	
1284	\leanok
1285	[Genus-$0$ curve $\cong \mathbb P^1$ over $\bar k$]
1286	  \label{prop:genusZero_curve_iso_P1}
1287	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.genusZero_curve_iso_P1}
1288	  \uses{def:genus}
1289	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne, Algebraic Geometry], Example IV.1.3.5, Ch.~IV \S1 (Riemann--Roch), doc p.~297 (read from references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 314)
1290	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Example 1.3.5. Recall that a curve is rational if it is birational to
1291	  % $\mathbf P^1$ (I, Ex. 6.1). Since curves in this chapter are complete and nonsingular by
1292	  % definition, a curve $X$ is rational if and only if $X \cong \mathbf P^1$ (I, 6.12). Now
1293	  % using (1.3) we can show that $X$ is rational if and only if $g = 0$. We already know that
1294	  % $p_a(\mathbf P^1) = 0$ (I, Ex. 7.2), so conversely suppose given a curve $X$ of genus 0.
1295	  % Let $P, Q$ be two distinct points of $X$ and apply Riemann-Roch to the divisor
1296	  % $D = P - Q$. Since $\deg(K - D) = -2$, using (1.3.3) above, we have $l(K - D) = 0$, and so
1297	  % we find $l(D) = 1$. But $D$ is a divisor of degree 0, so by (1.2) we have $D \sim 0$, in
1298	  % other words $P \sim Q$. But this implies $X$ is rational (II, 6.10.1)."
1299	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, Algebraic Geometry, Example~IV.1.3.5.}
1300	  Let $\bar k$ be algebraically closed and $C$ a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve
1301	  over $\bar k$ with $\genus(C) = 0$. Then $C \cong \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$.
1302	\end{proposition}
1303	
1304	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see the verbatim Example IV.1.3.5 text in the statement block above
1305	% (Hartshorne states the classification and its proof inside the single Example; the proof is
1306	% the second half beginning ``We already know that $p_a(\mathbf P^1) = 0$ \ldots'').
1307	\begin{proof}
1308	  \uses{def:genus}
1309	  \emph{Proof sketch (Riemann--Roch level; the formalisation of this step is itself a
1310	  sub-build --- Mathlib has no Riemann--Roch for curves).}
1311	
1312	  Over the algebraically closed field $\bar k$ every closed point is $\bar k$-rational, so $C$
1313	  has at least two distinct $\bar k$-points $P \neq Q$ (a smooth proper geometrically
1314	  irreducible curve over $\bar k$ is infinite). With $\genus(C) = \dim_{\bar k}
1315	  H^1(C, \mathcal O_C) = 0$, apply the Riemann--Roch theorem to the degree-$0$ divisor
1316	  $D = P - Q$. Since $\deg(K - D) = 2g - 2 - \deg D = -2 < 0$ one has $l(K - D) = 0$, so
1317	  Riemann--Roch $l(D) - l(K - D) = \deg D + 1 - g$ gives $l(D) = 0 + 1 - 0 = 1$. Thus the
1318	  degree-$0$ divisor $D$ is linearly equivalent to an effective divisor, forcing $D \sim 0$,
1319	  i.e.\ $P \sim Q$. A curve possessing two distinct linearly equivalent points is rational
1320	  (Hartshorne II.6.10.1: $P \sim Q$ for $P \neq Q$ yields a degree-$1$ map to $\mathbb P^1$),
1321	  and a complete nonsingular rational curve is isomorphic to $\mathbb P^1$ (Hartshorne I.6.12).
1322	  Hence $C \cong \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$.
1323	\end{proof}
1324	
1325	\begin{remark}
1326	  \label{rmk:genusZero_iso_subbuild}
1327	  \cref{prop:genusZero_curve_iso_P1} is Riemann--Roch--flavoured and its formalisation is a
1328	  genuine sub-build, not a short step: Mathlib has no Riemann--Roch theorem for curves, no
1329	  divisor class group machinery at this level, and no $\mathbb P^1$-classification of
1330	  rational curves. The statement is stated here at textbook level so the chain is complete;
1331	  closing its Lean proof will require either importing/porting a substantial slice of curve
1332	  theory or re-deriving the genus-$0 \Rightarrow \mathbb P^1$ classification from the
1333	  project's own $\genus$ definition. The dependency on $\genus$ (\cref{def:genus}) is the link
1334	  to \cref{chap:Genus}.
1335	\end{remark}
1336	
1337	\section{The headline: rigidity for a pointed genus-$0$ curve}
1338	
1339	\begin{theorem}
1340	
1341	\leanok
1342	[Rigidity: a pointed morphism from a genus-$0$ curve to an abelian variety is constant]
1343	  \label{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV}
1344	  \label{prop:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV}
1345	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme}
1346	  \uses{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant, prop:genusZero_curve_iso_P1}
1347	  Let $\bar k$ be an algebraically closed field (\emph{arbitrary characteristic}; no
1348	  $\mathtt{CharZero}$ hypothesis). Let $C$ be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve
1349	  over $\bar k$ with $\genus(C) = 0$, and let $A$ be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible
1350	  group scheme over $\bar k$ (an abelian variety) with identity $\eta_A$. Then for every
1351	  morphism $f \colon C \to A$ such that $p \fatsemi f = \eta_A$ for some $\bar k$-point
1352	  $p \colon \mathbf 1 \to C$, the morphism $f$ equals the constant morphism at the identity,
1353	  $f = \mathtt{toUnit}\,C \fatsemi \eta_A$.
1354	
1355	  This is the project's committed characteristic-free statement that the genus-$0$
1356	  Albanese-witness $\mathtt{genusZeroWitness}$ consumes. Its signature mirrors
1357	  $\mathtt{AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity\_over\_kbar}$ of \cref{chap:RigidityKbar}
1358	  \emph{verbatim except} that the $[\mathtt{CharZero}\ \bar k]$ instance is dropped: same
1359	  curve typeclasses ($\mathtt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}\ 1$, $\mathtt{IsProper}$,
1360	  $\mathtt{GeometricallyIrreducible}$ on $C$), same abelian-variety typeclasses
1361	  ($\mathtt{GrpObj}$, $\mathtt{IsProper}$, $\mathtt{Smooth}$, $\mathtt{GeometricallyIrreducible}$
1362	  on $A$), and the same pointed-morphism conclusion shape $f = \mathtt{toUnit}\,C \fatsemi
1363	  \eta[A]$.
1364	\end{theorem}
1365	
1366	\begin{proof}
1367	  \uses{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant, prop:genusZero_curve_iso_P1}
1368	  By \cref{prop:genusZero_curve_iso_P1} the genus-$0$ curve $C$ over the algebraically closed
1369	  field $\bar k$ is isomorphic to $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ via some isomorphism
1370	  $\varphi \colon C \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ } \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$. The composite
1371	  $f \circ \varphi^{-1} \colon \mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to A$ is then a morphism from
1372	  $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ to the abelian variety $A$, hence constant by
1373	  \cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}: its image is a single $\bar k$-point $a_0 \in A$.
1374	  Therefore $f$ itself is constant with value $a_0$. The pointed hypothesis
1375	  $p \fatsemi f = \eta_A$ evaluates this constant at the $\bar k$-point $p$ and pins it to the
1376	  identity, $a_0 = \eta_A$. Consequently $f$ factors through the terminal object as the
1377	  constant morphism at the identity, $f = \mathtt{toUnit}\,C \fatsemi \eta_A$.
1378	
1379	  No differential ($df = 0$) input, no $H^0(C, \Omega_C) = 0$ / Serre-duality decomposition,
1380	  and no Picard representability is used; the argument is valid in arbitrary characteristic.
1381	  Once formalised it lets the $[\mathtt{CharZero}\ \bar k]$ hypothesis carried by
1382	  $\mathtt{rigidity\_over\_kbar}$ be dropped, and (after the $\bar k \to k$ descent step hosted
1383	  in \cref{chap:Jacobian}, not here) feeds $\mathtt{genusZeroWitness}.\mathtt{key}$.
1384	\end{proof}
1385

6	import Mathlib

8	/-!
9	# Genus-`0` base objects: `ℙ¹`, `Ga`, `Gm`, and the `Gm`-scaling action `σ_×`
10	
11	This file ships the concrete genus-`0` base-case infrastructure (iter-165 lane) that
12	`AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity.morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` consumes via
13	the **`𝔾_m`-scaling shortcut** (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, Proposition 3.10): the total
14	scaling action `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾_m → ℙ¹`, `(x, λ) ↦ λx`, feeds the proven Cor 1.5
15	(`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity`); the `W`-axis collapses at the scaling fixed point
16	`0 ∈ ℙ¹`, giving `f(λx) = f(x)`; density of `𝔾_m ⊆ ℙ¹` plus `A` separated
17	(`ext_of_eqOnOpen`) force `f` constant. NO theorem of the cube, NO Milne Thm 3.2, NO
18	`Hom(𝔾_a, A) = 0`, char-general. See `blueprint/src/chapters/AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex`
19	(`def:genus0_base_objects`, `def:gaTranslationP1`).
20	
21	The file is **upstream** of `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean` (the mathlib-analogist
22	`gm-scaling-p1` D4 verdict: split into a focused file per scheme construction, mirroring
23	the precedent of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.{Smooth,Abelian}`). The AVR.lean refactor +
24	proof of `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` is iter-166's lane and does NOT happen here.
25	
26	## The four declarations
27	
28	1. `ProjectiveLineBar` — the projective line `ℙ¹` over `Spec k̄`, encoded as `Proj 𝒜` of
29	   the standard ℕ-graded `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄`, viewed as an object of
30	   `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`. `IsProper` is FREE from
31	   `AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.instIsProperToSpecZero…` (the algebra is finite type).
32	   `GeometricallyIrreducible` and `SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1` are project-side sub-builds
33	   (Mathlib does not ship these for `Proj`), left as scaffold `sorry`s for iter-166+.
34	
35	2. `Ga` — the additive group object `𝔾_a` over `Spec k̄`, encoded as
36	   `(AffineSpace (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))).asOver _`. `IsAffine` is FREE; `GrpObj` is
37	   installed via `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` with the additive-group functor
38	   `T ↦ AddGrpCat.of Γ(T.left, ⊤)`; `Smooth` is FREE from
39	   `smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed`.
40	
41	3. `Gm` — the multiplicative group object `𝔾_m` over `Spec k̄`, encoded as
42	   `(Spec (.of (Localization.Away (X : MvPolynomial Unit k̄)))).asOver _`
43	   (= `Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]`, AFFINE — NOT the basic-open path). `IsAffine` is FREE; `GrpObj` is
44	   installed via `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` with the units functor
45	   `T ↦ GrpCat.of Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ`; `Smooth` is FREE from
46	   `smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed`.
47	
48	4. `gmScalingP1 : ProjectiveLineBar ⊗ Gm ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar` — the bare scaling
49	   morphism `(x, λ) ↦ λx`, with companion lemma `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero` exposing
50	   the load-bearing fixed-point property at `0 ∈ ℙ¹` that `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity`
51	   (Cor 1.5) needs.
52	
53	The blueprint chapter `AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex` covers this file via
54	`% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean
55	AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects.lean`.
56	-/

58	set_option autoImplicit false
59	set_option linter.style.setOption false
60	
61	universe u
62	
63	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
64	
65	noncomputable section
66	
67	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
68	
69	/-! ### (A) The projective line `ℙ¹` over `Spec k̄` (the `Proj` realisation) -/
70	
71	/-- The **standard ℕ-grading on `k̄[X₀, X₁]`** by total degree.
72	
73	This is the homogeneous-component decomposition `MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule`
74	specialised to two variables (`Fin 2`) over `k̄`. The `GradedRing` instance is
75	`MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra` (free).
76	
77	Used to define `ProjectiveLineBar = Proj` of this graded ring. -/
78	abbrev projectiveLineBarGrading (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
79	    ℕ → Submodule kbar (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
80	  MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar
81	
82	instance projectiveLineBarGrading_gradedRing (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
83	    GradedRing (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) :=
84	  MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra
85	
86	/-- **The projective line over `Spec k̄` as a scheme.** This is `Proj 𝒜` of the standard
87	ℕ-graded `k̄[X₀, X₁]`. Carries an `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance via
88	`Proj.toSpecZero` composed with the algebra-map identification of the degree-`0` piece
89	with `k̄` (Mathlib's `SetLike.GradeZero.instAlgebraSubtypeMemOfNat`).
90	
91	`IsProper` is FREE — see the `instIsProper` instance below. `IsAlgClosed kbar` is *not*
92	required for the scheme itself but is needed for downstream `Smooth`/geometric
93	irreducibility instances. -/
94	def ProjectiveLineBarScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
95	  Proj (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
96	
97	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` structure on `ProjectiveLineBarScheme` via
98	`Proj.toSpecZero` and the identification `k̄ ≃ ↥(𝒜 0)`. -/
99	instance projectiveLineBarScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
100	    (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) where
101	  hom := Proj.toSpecZero (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ≫
102	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
103	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0)))
104	
105	/-- **The projective line `ℙ¹_{k̄}` as an object of `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** This is the
106	concrete scheme used by `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` (iter-166) to formalise the
107	`𝔾_m`-scaling shortcut. -/
108	def ProjectiveLineBar (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
109	  (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
110	
111	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is proper over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
112	`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.instIsProperToSpecZero…` — the algebra `k̄[X₀, X₁]` is finite type
113	over its degree-`0` piece `↥(𝒜 0) ≃ k̄` — chained via the standard properness-of-composition
114	lemma with the `Spec.map` of the algebra map `k̄ → ↥(𝒜 0)` (which is bijective hence
115	gives an iso of `Spec`s).
116	
117	The chain:
118	* `Proj.toSpecZero 𝒜` is proper (Mathlib's
119	  `Proj.instIsProperToSpecZeroOfFiniteTypeSubtypeMemOfNatNat`, given
120	  `Algebra.FiniteType ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄)` — supplied by the
121	  `IsScalarTower kbar ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial _ _)` + `Algebra.FiniteType k̄ (MvPolynomial _ _)`
122	  chain via `Algebra.FiniteType.of_restrictScalars_finiteType`);
123	* `Spec.map (algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0))` is an iso because `algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0)` is bijective
124	  in this standard ℕ-grading case (`𝒜 0 = MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) k̄ 0`
125	  is the constants subalgebra, naturally ≅ k̄);
126	* composition of proper + iso is proper. -/
127	instance projectiveLineBar_isProper (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
128	    IsProper (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom := by
129	  -- Unfold to expose the Proj.toSpecZero ≫ Spec.map chain.
130	  change IsProper (Proj.toSpecZero (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ≫
131	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
132	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))))
133	  -- The IsScalarTower kbar ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial _ kbar) needed for the FiniteType derivation.
134	  haveI : IsScalarTower kbar
135	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)
136	      (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
137	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun _ => rfl
138	  -- MvPolynomial.{Fin 2} k̄ is finite type over ↥(𝒜 0) (chained from finite-type over k̄).
139	  haveI : Algebra.FiniteType
140	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)
141	      (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
142	    Algebra.FiniteType.of_restrictScalars_finiteType kbar _ _
143	  -- The algebra map `k̄ → ↥(𝒜 0)` is bijective: the degree-`0` piece of the homogeneous
144	  -- decomposition is exactly the constants `C(k̄) ⊆ MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄`. Injectivity
145	  -- follows from `MvPolynomial.C_injective`. Surjectivity uses
146	  -- `MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem` (which says the degree-`0` component of a
147	  -- homogeneous-degree-`0` polynomial is itself) plus `homogeneousComponent_zero`
148	  -- (which expresses the degree-`0` component as `C` of the constant coefficient).
149	  have hbij : Function.Bijective (algebraMap kbar
150	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)) := by
151	    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
152	    · intro x y h
153	      apply MvPolynomial.C_injective (Fin 2) kbar
154	      exact congrArg Subtype.val h
155	    · intro ⟨v, hv⟩
156	      refine ⟨MvPolynomial.coeff 0 v, ?_⟩
157	      apply Subtype.ext
158	      rw [SetLike.GradeZero.coe_algebraMap]
159	      have key := MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem hv (m := 0)
160	      -- `key : (homogeneousComponent 0) v = if 0 = 0 then v else 0` which simp resolves
161	      -- and combines with `homogeneousComponent_zero` to give `C (coeff 0 v) = v`.
162	      simp only [MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_zero, if_true] at key
163	      exact key
164	  -- Therefore `Spec.map (algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0))` is iso, and composition of proper + iso is
165	  -- proper.
166	  haveI : IsIso (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
167	      (algebraMap kbar ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)))) := by
168	    rw [isIso_SpecMap_iff]
169	    exact hbij
170	  infer_instance
171

213	/-! ### (B) The additive group `𝔾_a` over `Spec k̄` -/

215	/-- **The additive group `𝔾_a = 𝔸¹` over `Spec k̄` as an underlying scheme.** This is the
216	affine line `AffineSpace (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))`. It is affine, locally of finite
217	presentation, and reduced (its global sections are `MvPolynomial (Fin 1) k̄`, a domain). -/
218	def GaScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=

228	`Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** -/

270	    Smooth (Ga kbar).hom :=

274	/-! ### (C) The multiplicative group `𝔾_m` over `Spec k̄` -/
275	
276	/-- **The ring `k̄[t, t⁻¹] = Localization.Away t`**, where `t = X () : MvPolynomial Unit k̄`.
277	This is the global-sections ring of `𝔾_m`. -/
278	abbrev GmRing (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Type u :=
279	  Localization.Away (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)
280	
281	/-- **The multiplicative group `𝔾_m = Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]` as an underlying scheme.** This is
282	the chosen affine encoding (the analogist `gm-scaling-p1` D2.b verdict: AFFINE `Spec`, NOT
283	the basic-open of `𝔸¹` — the latter loses `IsAffine`). -/
284	def GmScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
285	  Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))
286	
287	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance on `GmScheme`. -/
288	instance gmScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
289	    (GmScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) where
290	  hom := Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)))
291	
292	/-- **The multiplicative group object `𝔾_m` over `Spec k̄` as an object of
293	`Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** -/
294	abbrev Gm (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
295	  (GmScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
296	
297	/-- **`𝔾_m` is affine.** -/
298	instance gm_isAffine (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsAffine (Gm kbar).left :=
299	  inferInstanceAs (IsAffine (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))))
300	
301	/-- **`𝔾_m` is locally of finite presentation over `Spec k̄`.** Follows from
302	`Algebra.FinitePresentation k̄ (k̄[t, t⁻¹])` (a localization of a polynomial ring at a single
303	element is finitely presented), bridged through
304	`AlgebraicGeometry.HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff` for the `LocallyOfFinitePresentation` /
305	`RingHom.FinitePresentation` correspondence. -/

308	  (HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff (P := @LocallyOfFinitePresentation)).mpr
309	    ((RingHom.finitePresentation_algebraMap (B := GmRing kbar)).mpr
310	      inferInstance)
311	
312	/-- **`𝔾_m`'s underlying scheme is reduced.** `k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is a localization of a domain at
313	a non-zero-divisor, hence a domain, hence reduced. The `IsReduced (Spec _)` instance picks
314	up directly from `IsReduced (Localization.Away _)`. -/
315	instance gm_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsReduced (Gm kbar).left :=

318	/-- **`GrpObj`-structure on `𝔾_m` via `ofRepresentableBy`.**

328	`W = Gm`). -/
329	instance gm_grpObj (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : GrpObj (Gm kbar) := sorry
330	
331	/-- **`𝔾_m` is smooth over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from `smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed` once
332	`GrpObj`, `LocallyOfFinitePresentation`, and `IsReduced` are installed. -/
333	instance gm_smooth (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar] :
334	    Smooth (Gm kbar).hom :=

336	  smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed (Gm kbar).hom

338	/-- The `k̄`-point `1 ∈ 𝔾_m` (the multiplicative identity), encoded as the group-object
339	unit `η[Gm kbar] : 𝟙_ ⟶ Gm kbar`. -/
340	def Gm.onePt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
341	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ Gm kbar :=
342	  η[Gm kbar]

344	/-! ### (D) The `𝔾_m`-scaling action `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹`
345	
346	`gmScalingP1` is a *bare* `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`-morphism (the analogist D3 verdict:
347	no `IsAction`/`MulAction`-style typeclass at scheme level — Mathlib has no such precedent;
348	the rigidity consumer needs only the bare morphism + the named fixed-point lemma).
349	
350	Chartwise definition: on `𝔸¹ × 𝔾_m` (target chart `D₊(X₀)` of `ℙ¹`), the morphism is
351	the polynomial map `(x, λ) ↦ λx`; near `∞` (target chart `D₊(X₁)`, coordinate `u = 1/x`),
352	the target coordinate `1/(λx) = u/λ` is regular because `λ ∈ 𝔾_m` is invertible. The two
353	chart-restrictions agree on `(𝔸¹ ∖ {0}) × 𝔾_m`, so they glue via
354	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms`.
355	
356	The companion lemma `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero` exposes the load-bearing fixed-point
357	property `σ_×(0, λ) = 0` for all `λ ∈ 𝔾_m`, packaged as the `W`-axis-collapse hypothesis
358	that `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` (Cor 1.5) consumes. -/
359	
360	/-- **The `𝔾_m`-scaling action `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹`** in `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.
361	
362	Scaffold body — iter-165 lands the *type signature* (the concrete object the iter-166
363	proof refactor of `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` consumes); the chartwise glue body
364	is iter-166's lane (or an even later sub-build if `Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms` requires
365	further infrastructure). -/
366	def gmScalingP1 (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
367	    ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⊗ Gm kbar ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
368	  sorry
369	
370	/-- **The load-bearing fixed-point property of `σ_×`:** at the scaling fixed point
371	`0 ∈ ℙ¹`, the morphism `σ_×(0, ·) : 𝔾_m → ℙ¹` is the constant morphism at `0`. That is,
372	the composite `(0 ≫ toUnit) × 𝟙 : 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹` equals `toUnit ≫ 0`.
373	
374	This is precisely the `W`-axis-collapse hypothesis `_hf` that
375	`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` (Cor 1.5) consumes when applied with `V = ℙ¹` proper,
376	`W = 𝔾_m`, base points `0 ∈ ℙ¹`, `1 ∈ 𝔾_m`.
377	
378	Scaffold body — iter-165 lands the *statement* (matching the rigidity consumer's `_hf`
379	shape); the proof body is iter-166's lane (it reduces to a chart-level computation: on
380	`𝔸¹ × 𝔾_m`, `(0, λ) ↦ λ·0 = 0` is a defequal ring-map check). -/
381	lemma gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
382	    lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar)) ≫
383	        gmScalingP1 kbar =
384	      toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar := by
385	  sorry
386	
387	end AlgebraicGeometry
388	
389	end

813:theorem hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity
873:`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` consumes with `V = W = A`). If a regular map `α : A ⟶ B` sends the
877:PROOF (Milne, Cor 1.2, §I.1). Apply the additive decomposition `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity`
883:theorem av_regularMap_isHom_of_zero
899:  have key := hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity (V := A) (W := A) (A := B)
917:set `h := σ_× ≫ f`, and apply the proven Cor 1.5 (`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity`, with

800	along the projections:
801	
802	  `h = (p ≫ f) · (q ≫ g)`,
803	
804	where `p = fst V W`, `q = snd V W`, `f = (v ↦ (v, w₀)) ≫ h` is `h|_{V × {w₀}}`, and
805	`g = (w ↦ (v₀, w)) ≫ h` is `h|_{{v₀} × W}`. The operation `·` is the hom-group multiplication
806	(`u * v = lift u v ≫ μ`); the statement does **not** assume `A` commutative.
807	
808	PROOF (Milne, Cor 1.5, §I.1). Form the group difference `φ := h / ((p ≫ f) · (q ≫ g))`. A direct
809	hom-group computation shows `φ` collapses the complete `V`-axis `V × {w₀}` to the identity (this is
810	exactly the `_hf` collapse hypothesis of `rigidity_lemma`, using `h(v₀, w₀) = η[A]`), so by the
811	Rigidity Lemma `φ = q ≫ g'` factors through the second projection; `φ` also vanishes on the
812	section `{v₀} × W` of `q`, forcing `g' = 1`, hence `φ = 1` and `h = (p ≫ f) · (q ≫ g)`. -/
813	theorem hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity
814	    [IsAlgClosed kbar]
815	    {V W : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
816	    [IsProper V.hom]
817	    [GeometricallyIrreducible (V ⊗ W).hom]
818	    [LocallyOfFiniteType (V ⊗ W).hom]
819	    [IsReduced (V ⊗ W).left]
820	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
821	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom]
822	    (v₀ : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ V)
823	    (w₀ : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ W)
824	    (h : V ⊗ W ⟶ A)
825	    (hh : lift v₀ w₀ ≫ h = η[A]) :
826	    h = (fst V W ≫ (lift (𝟙 V) (toUnit V ≫ w₀) ≫ h)) *
827	        (snd V W ≫ (lift (toUnit W ≫ v₀) (𝟙 W) ≫ h)) := by
828	  haveI : IsSeparated A.hom := inferInstance
829	  -- Name the two axis-restrictions (folding the goal's RHS).
830	  set f : V ⟶ A := lift (𝟙 V) (toUnit V ≫ w₀) ≫ h with hf
831	  set g : W ⟶ A := lift (toUnit W ≫ v₀) (𝟙 W) ≫ h with hg
832	  -- Projection/section identities for the two slice sections.
833	  have hsVfst : lift (𝟙 V) (toUnit V ≫ w₀) ≫ fst V W = 𝟙 V := by simp
834	  have hsVsnd : lift (𝟙 V) (toUnit V ≫ w₀) ≫ snd V W = toUnit V ≫ w₀ := by simp
835	  have hsWfst : lift (toUnit W ≫ v₀) (𝟙 W) ≫ fst V W = toUnit W ≫ v₀ := by simp
836	  have hsWsnd : lift (toUnit W ≫ v₀) (𝟙 W) ≫ snd V W = 𝟙 W := by simp
837	  -- The cross-restrictions of `h` are the identity, because `h(v₀, w₀) = η[A]`.
838	  have hwsW : w₀ ≫ lift (toUnit W ≫ v₀) (𝟙 W) = lift v₀ w₀ := by
839	    rw [comp_lift, Category.comp_id, ← Category.assoc,
840	      toUnit_unique (w₀ ≫ toUnit W) (𝟙 _), Category.id_comp]
841	  have hvsV : v₀ ≫ lift (𝟙 V) (toUnit V ≫ w₀) = lift v₀ w₀ := by
842	    rw [comp_lift, Category.comp_id, ← Category.assoc,
843	      toUnit_unique (v₀ ≫ toUnit V) (𝟙 _), Category.id_comp]
844	  have hwg : w₀ ≫ g = η[A] := by rw [hg, ← Category.assoc, hwsW, hh]
845	  have hvf : v₀ ≫ f = η[A] := by rw [hf, ← Category.assoc, hvsV, hh]
846	  -- The group difference `φ = h / (f∘p · g∘q)`.
847	  set φ : V ⊗ W ⟶ A := h / ((fst V W ≫ f) * (snd V W ≫ g)) with hφ
848	  -- `φ` collapses the complete `V`-axis `V × {w₀}` to the identity.
849	  have hcolV : lift (𝟙 V) (toUnit V ≫ w₀) ≫ φ = toUnit V ≫ η[A] := by
850	    rw [← Hom.one_def, hφ, GrpObj.comp_div, ← hf, MonObj.comp_mul,
851	      ← Category.assoc, hsVfst, Category.id_comp,
852	      ← Category.assoc, hsVsnd, Category.assoc, hwg, ← Hom.one_def, _root_.mul_one, div_self']
853	  -- `φ` vanishes on the `{v₀} × W` section of the second projection.
854	  have hcolW : lift (toUnit W ≫ v₀) (𝟙 W) ≫ φ = (1 : W ⟶ A) := by
855	    rw [hφ, GrpObj.comp_div, ← hg, MonObj.comp_mul,
856	      ← Category.assoc, hsWfst, Category.assoc, hvf, ← Hom.one_def,
857	      ← Category.assoc, hsWsnd, Category.id_comp, _root_.one_mul, div_self']
858	  -- Rigidity: `φ` factors through the second projection.
859	  obtain ⟨g', hg'⟩ := rigidity_lemma φ v₀ w₀ η[A] hcolV
860	  -- The factor is the identity, read off the `{v₀} × W` section.
861	  have hg'1 : g' = 1 := by
862	    have hsec : lift (toUnit W ≫ v₀) (𝟙 W) ≫ φ = g' := by
863	      rw [hg', ← Category.assoc, hsWsnd, Category.id_comp]
864	    rw [← hsec, hcolW]
865	  have hφ1 : φ = 1 := by rw [hg', hg'1, MonObj.comp_one]
866	  -- Conclude `h = f∘p · g∘q`.
867	  have hdiv : h / ((fst V W ≫ f) * (snd V W ≫ g)) = 1 := by rw [← hφ]; exact hφ1
868	  exact div_eq_one.mp hdiv
869	
870	/-- **A pointed regular map of abelian varieties is a homomorphism (Milne Corollary 1.2).** Let
871	`A`, `B` be abelian varieties over an algebraically closed `k̄` (with `A ⊗ A` a variety —
872	geometrically irreducible, reduced, locally of finite type, the hypotheses
873	`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` consumes with `V = W = A`). If a regular map `α : A ⟶ B` sends the
874	identity to the identity (`η[A] ≫ α = η[B]`), then `α` is a monoid (hence group) homomorphism:
875	`IsMonHom α`, i.e. `α(a · a') = α(a) · α(a')`.
876	
877	PROOF (Milne, Cor 1.2, §I.1). Apply the additive decomposition `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity`
878	(Cor 1.5) to `h := μ[A] ≫ α : A ⊗ A ⟶ B`, based at `η[A]` (the hypothesis `h(η, η) = η[B]` is
879	`η[A] ≫ α = η[B]` since `η[A]` is the hom-group identity). The two axis-restrictions of `h` both
880	collapse to `α` (by the monoid unit laws `lift_comp_one_right`/`left`: `(a, η) ↦ a` and
881	`(η, a) ↦ a`), so the decomposition reads `μ[A] ≫ α = (fst ≫ α) · (snd ≫ α) = (α ⊗ α) ≫ μ[B]`,
882	which is exactly the `mul_hom` axiom; `one_hom` is the pointed hypothesis. -/
883	theorem av_regularMap_isHom_of_zero
884	    [IsAlgClosed kbar]
885	    {A B : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
886	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
887	    [GeometricallyIrreducible (A ⊗ A).hom]
888	    [LocallyOfFiniteType (A ⊗ A).hom]
889	    [IsReduced (A ⊗ A).left]
890	    [GrpObj B] [IsProper B.hom]
891	    (α : A ⟶ B) (hα : η[A] ≫ α = η[B]) :
892	    IsMonHom α := by
893	  -- `η[A]` is the identity of the hom-group `Hom(𝟙_, A)`, so `η[A] · η[A] = η[A]`.
894	  have h1 : (η[A] : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ A) = 1 := by
895	    rw [Hom.one_def, toUnit_unique (toUnit _) (𝟙 _), Category.id_comp]
896	  have hbase : lift η[A] η[A] ≫ μ[A] = η[A] := by
897	    rw [← Hom.mul_def, h1, _root_.mul_one]
898	  -- Corollary 1.5 applied to `h := μ[A] ≫ α` with `V = W = A`, based at `η[A]`.
899	  have key := hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity (V := A) (W := A) (A := B)
900	    η[A] η[A] (μ[A] ≫ α) (by rw [← Category.assoc, hbase, hα])
901	  -- Both axis-restrictions of `μ[A] ≫ α` reduce to `α` (monoid unit laws).
902	  rw [show lift (𝟙 A) (toUnit A ≫ η[A]) ≫ μ[A] ≫ α = α by
903	        rw [← Category.assoc, lift_comp_one_right, Category.id_comp],
904	      show lift (toUnit A ≫ η[A]) (𝟙 A) ≫ μ[A] ≫ α = α by
905	        rw [← Category.assoc, lift_comp_one_left, Category.id_comp]] at key
906	  -- `key : μ[A] ≫ α = (fst A A ≫ α) · (snd A A ≫ α)`; package as `IsMonHom`.
907	  exact { one_hom := hα, mul_hom := by rw [key, Hom.mul_def, lift_fst_comp_snd_comp] }

# progress-critic directive (iter-166, slug `routec166`)

## Task

ahead of the iter-166 prover dispatch. The planner is deciding whether to fire two
parallel lanes — `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean` refactor + proof close, and
`Genus0BaseObjects.lean` body closes. The iter-164 critic gave Route C as `CONVERGING`
with a watch tripwire for iter-166: "if iter-166 does not show the AVR refactor / proof
body started, verdict flips to `CHURNING`." We need a fresh read on whether
the planner's iter-166 lane choice avoids the tripwire and whether the route is still
converging given the iter-165 signals.

## Routes and last-K signals (K = 5, iter-161 through iter-165)

### Route C — genus-0 base case via 𝔾_m-scaling shortcut

`AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects.lean` (NEW iter-165),
`AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean` (consumer; gated).

| 162  | AVR.lean | COMPLETE | Rigidity Lemma whole-chain closed |
| 163  | AVR.lean | COMPLETE | Cor 1.5 + Cor 1.2 proven |

**Recurring blocker phrases:** none — no "Mathlib doesn't have it" / "deferred to next iter
because X" / "couldn't unify" patterns across iters 161–165. The iter-165 PARTIAL
outcome is plan-allowed (the PARTIAL gate scorecard explicitly authorized scaffold
sorries on `gmScalingP1`, `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero`, `gm_grpObj`, etc., naming them
as iter-166 lanes).

### STRATEGY current estimate for Route C base-case sub-phase

From `STRATEGY.md` `## Phases & estimations` (iter-164 cell, unchanged iter-165):

| Phase | Status | Iters left | LOC (remaining · realized/it) | Risks |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| genus-0 rigidity | Rigidity chain + Cor 1.5 + Cor 1.2 CLOSED axiom-clean; base case route RESOLVED = 𝔾_m-scaling shortcut | ~10–18 | ~2000–4000 · chain+Cor done, base-case 0/it | concrete ℙ¹/𝔾_m + σ_× new infra (elementary, char-free); RR bridge has no Mathlib support |

Phase entered current sub-phase iter-164 (route resolution). Elapsed in this sub-phase:
2 iters (164, 165). Iters left (per STRATEGY): 10–18.

### Planner's iter-166 `## Current Objectives` proposal

TWO parallel prover lanes:

1. **`AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`** —
   - `import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects`;
   - refactor `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` (currently L927-936, abstract `P1`-proxy)
     to the concrete `ProjectiveLineBar`-signature (statement becomes "for all
     `f : ProjectiveLineBar ⟶ A`, ∃ `a₀`, `f = toUnit ≫ a₀`");
   - prove its body via the proven `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` (Cor 1.5) +
     `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero` (as `_hf`) + density `Gm ⊆ ProjectiveLineBar` +
     `ext_of_eqOnOpen` (proven, `Rigidity.lean`) — full proof chain detailed in

     `rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` can transport via the iso).

   sorries that Lane 1 references as black boxes:
   - `gm_grpObj` (`GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` with units functor; consumed by Cor 1.5);
   - `gmScalingP1` body (chartwise glue via `Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms`);
   - `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero` body (chart-level computation);
   - `ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt` body (`Proj.awayι ≫ Spec.map` for `[0 : 1]`);
   - optionally: `ProjectiveLineBar.onePt`, `ProjectiveLineBar.inftyPt`, the GeometricallyIrreducible
     + SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 sub-builds if time permits.

Lane 1 and Lane 2 are file-disjoint (parallel-safe). Lane 1's proof body uses Lane 2's
signatures as black boxes — even if Lane 2 ships PARTIAL, Lane 1's theorem still
type-checks (propagates `sorryAx` through the upstream scaffolds until they close).

1. **Watch-tripwire compliance.** The iter-164 critic's tripwire was "iter-166 must
   show AVR refactor / proof body started, else CHURNING." Does the planner's
   2-lane dispatch (Lane 1 = the AVR refactor + proof body) satisfy the tripwire?
   If yes, certify Route C as CONVERGING. If no — i.e. you read Lane 1 as still
   "scaffolding"-shaped or planner is dispatching a 3rd cosmetic round — name the
   gap.

   sequential lane (Lane 2 first to unblock Lane 1)? Note that the AVR signature
   refactor only consumes Lane 2's SIGNATURES (already landed iter-165), not the
   bodies, so the lanes are formally independent.

3. **Reversal signal.** If Lane 1 fails (the proof body of
   `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` does not close this iter), what is the cheapest
   signal that the route needs corrective action? My read: a Lane 1 failure with
   blocker phrase "instance synthesis at the V/W slots of Cor 1.5" would suggest
   the abstract→concrete refactor is the issue, not the math.

4. **Iters-left consistency check.** STRATEGY says ~10–18 iters left for genus-0
   rigidity at ~chain+Cor done, base-case 0/it velocity. iter-165 added 4 main
   objects + 13 instances/defs — that's a substantial velocity jump (from 0/it to
   ~17 instances/defs). Should the planner re-estimate? My read is yes: if Lane 1
   closes iter-166 and Lane 2 mostly closes iter-167, the base case is done in
   ~3 iters from iter-164's route-resolution — well under 10. But the
   `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` Riemann-Roch sub-build remains, which the STRATEGY
   row already flags as "RR bridge has no Mathlib support".

## Required output sections

- `## Verdict per route` — CONVERGING / CHURNING / STUCK / UNCLEAR for Route C.
- `## Tripwire compliance` — answer Q1 explicitly (yes/no + the evidence).
- `## Lane count` — answer Q2.
- `## Reversal signals` — answer Q3.
- `## Iters-left re-estimation` — answer Q4 (recommend a refresh number or "keep").
- `## Recommended correctives (if non-CONVERGING)` — name the corrective TYPE
  (blueprint expansion / Mathlib-idiom consult / structural refactor / route pivot);
  the planner picks the matching subagent.

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus
7	
8	/-!
9	# Abelian-variety rigidity: morphisms from a genus-`0` curve into an abelian variety are constant
10	
11	This file scaffolds the project's **committed characteristic-free** genus-`0` route
12	(route (c)): the abelian-variety rigidity stack. It sits **upstream** of
13	`AlgebraicJacobian.Jacobian` (it imports only `AlgebraicJacobian.Genus`), breaking the
14	`RigidityKbar → Rigidity → Jacobian` import cycle so that `genusZeroWitness` can consume the
15	genus-`0` rigidity keystone directly.
16	
17	The headline `rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` is the char-free replacement (no
18	`[CharZero kbar]`) for `AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_over_kbar` of `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityKbar`
19	(which remains in tree as the fallback route (a) artifact and still carries `[CharZero]`).
20	
21	The minimal chain has four links:
22	
23	1. `rigidity_lemma` — the Rigidity Lemma (Mumford, Form I): the cube-free, cohomology-free
24	   properness/closed-map entry point. **PROVEN axiom-clean** (iters 157–162). The whole chain is
25	   `sorry`-free: bridge 1 is `snd_left_isClosedMap`; bridge 2
26	   (`rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine`, the slice-constancy / affine-constancy equation) is
27	   assembled from Step 2 `morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints` and Step 1
28	   `rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine`, whose deep *algebraic* content is
29	   `eq_comp_of_isAffine_of_properIntegral` and whose *geometric* slice/section assembly (the
30	   `IsIntegral X.left` retract argument, `isIntegral_of_retract`) is also closed.
31	2. `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` — every morphism `ℙ¹ → A` into an abelian variety is
32	   constant. Proved (route resolved iter-164) by the **𝔾ₘ-scaling shortcut**: the total scaling
33	   action `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾ₘ → ℙ¹`, `(x, λ) ↦ λx`, feeds the proven Cor 1.5
34	   (`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity`) — NO theorem of the cube, NO Milne Thm 3.2, NO
35	   `Hom(𝔾ₐ, A) = 0`, char-general. (Still a scaffold `sorry` pending the concrete ℙ¹/𝔾ₘ/σ_× infra.)
36	3. `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` — a smooth proper geom-irred genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is
37	   isomorphic to `ℙ¹` (blocked on Riemann–Roch).
38	4. `rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` — THE HEADLINE consumed by `genusZeroWitness`.
39	
40	See `blueprint/src/chapters/AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex` for the informal sketches and sources
41	(Mumford, *Abelian Varieties*, Ch. II §4, §6; Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, Prop. 3.10;
42	Hartshorne, *Algebraic Geometry*, Example IV.1.3.5).
43	
44	## Encoding notes (iter-157 scaffold)
45	
46	Mathlib `b80f227` packages no `ℙ¹` as a `Scheme`, so — following the established project idiom
47	(see `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityKbar`) — the projective line is encoded by its abstract
48	characterisation: a smooth proper geometrically irreducible `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` of relative
49	dimension `1` with `genus = 0`. The signatures of declarations 1–3 are **provisional**
50	(`SCAFFOLD` comments mark them); the prover may refine the encoding when the bodies are filled.
51	Declaration 4 is pinned verbatim to `rigidity_over_kbar`'s signature minus `[CharZero kbar]`,
52	because it is the exact signature the consumer (`genusZeroWitness.key`) needs.
53	-/
54	
55	set_option autoImplicit false
56	
57	universe u
58	
59	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
60	
61	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
62	
63	variable {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar]
64	
65	/-- **Cartesian-monoidal identity (skeleton step of the Rigidity Lemma).** Post-composing the
66	second projection `snd : X ⊗ Y ⟶ Y` with the slice section `y ↦ (x₀, y)` is the "collapse the
67	`X`-axis onto `x₀`" endomorphism `(x, y) ↦ (x₀, y)` of `X ⊗ Y`:
68	`snd ≫ lift (toUnit Y ≫ x₀) (𝟙 Y) = lift (toUnit (X ⊗ Y) ≫ x₀) (snd X Y)`.
69	
70	Pure cartesian-monoidal algebra (no geometry): `comp_lift` distributes the `snd`, the
71	`𝟙 Y` component simplifies by `Category.comp_id`, and the `toUnit Y` component collapses by
72	uniqueness of maps into the terminal object. -/
73	theorem rigidity_snd_lift
74	    {X Y : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
75	    (x₀ : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ X) :
76	    snd X Y ≫ lift (toUnit Y ≫ x₀) (𝟙 Y) =
77	      lift (toUnit (X ⊗ Y) ≫ x₀) (snd X Y) := by
78	  ext1 <;> simp
79	
80	/-- **Bridge 1 of the Rigidity Lemma (closed-map step), PROVEN.** When `X` is complete (proper)
81	over `k̄`, the second monoidal projection `snd : X ⊗ Y ⟶ Y` has, on underlying schemes, a
82	*closed* base map. This is Mumford's "completeness of `X` makes `p₂` a closed map" (Abelian
83	Varieties, Ch. II §4, p. 43).
84	
85	Proof: the underlying scheme morphism `(snd X Y).left` is the pullback projection
86	`Limits.pullback.snd X.hom Y.hom` (`Over.snd_left`), i.e. the base change of `X.hom` along
87	`Y.hom`. `IsProper X.hom ⟹ UniversallyClosed X.hom` (`IsProper.toUniversallyClosed`), and
88	`UniversallyClosed` is stable under base change
89	(`universallyClosed_isStableUnderBaseChange.of_isPullback` on the canonical pullback square), so
90	`(snd X Y).left` is universally closed and hence its base map is closed

# Iter-166 plan-agent run

## Headline outcome

**The "AVR refactor + scaffold-close" iter** — iter-166 picks up the iter-165 depth-conversion
scaffold and converts it into closed structural content. Two parallel prover lanes:

1. **`AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`** — import `Genus0BaseObjects`; refactor
   `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` to the concrete `ProjectiveLineBar` signature; prove the body
   via the proven `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` (Cor 1.5) consuming
   `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero` as the `W`-axis-collapse `_hf`, then density of `Gm ⊆ ℙ¹`
   + `A` separated (`ext_of_eqOnOpen`, proven). Refactor `genusZero_curve_iso_P1`'s target to
   `ProjectiveLineBar` so `rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` can transport.
2. **`Genus0BaseObjects.lean`** — close the LIVE-CONSUMER scaffold sorries Lane 1 references as
   black boxes: `gm_grpObj`, `gmScalingP1` body, `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero` body, and the three
   `ProjectiveLineBar` k̄-points (`zeroPt`, `onePt`, `inftyPt`). Optionally also
   `projectiveLineBar_geomIrred` / `projectiveLineBar_smoothOfRelDim` if time permits (sub-builds
   Mathlib does not ship for `Proj`).

The lanes are **file-disjoint** (parallel-safe). Lane 1's proof body consumes only Lane 2's
SIGNATURES (already landed iter-165), not bodies — so even if Lane 2 ships PARTIAL, Lane 1's
theorem still type-checks, propagating `sorryAx` upward through the scaffolds that remain.

## Decision made (lane count + signature refactor)

**2 parallel lanes, signature of `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` refactored from the
abstract `(P1 : Over (Spec k̄))` proxy to the concrete `ProjectiveLineBar kbar`.**

Reasoning:
- The progress-critic `routec166` verdict is CONVERGING with the iter-164 tripwire ("iter-166
  must show AVR refactor / proof body started, else CHURNING") **satisfied**: Lane 1 IS the
  refactor + proof body. Going to 2 lanes at exactly the iter the second lane becomes ready
  (Genus0BaseObjects.lean shipped iter-165) is the dispatch-sanity sweet spot — not under-dispatch,
  not over-dispatch.
- Signature refactor (abstract `P1` ⟶ concrete `ProjectiveLineBar`): the abstract proxy was a
  pre-iter-164 device when no concrete ℙ¹ existed in the project. Now it does, so the proof can
  directly consume `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero`'s exact shape. Keeping the abstract proxy would
  force a transport-via-iso step that is gratuitous (and circular: the iso to ℙ¹ is precisely
  `genusZero_curve_iso_P1`, which we're also refactoring on this iter). The downstream consumer
  `rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` uses `genusZero_curve_iso_P1 → C ≅ ProjectiveLineBar` to
  transport its hypothesis-curve to ℙ¹, then applies the concrete
  `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const`.

**Cheapest reversal signal** (per progress-critic Q3): a Lane 1 failure with the blocker phrase
"instance synthesis fails at the V/W slots of `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity`" or
"`gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero` statement doesn't match `_hf` literally" — both of these are
structural-shape issues (re-align signatures), NOT a route pivot. Lane 1's math has been
validated in the blueprint chapter (AVR.tex L1199-1278). If the prover hits issue (3) of the
critic's list — the `_hf` shape doesn't unify — iter-167 fixes the lemma statement in one shot.

## Prior critique status

- **progress-critic `routec` iter-165 tripwire ("iter-166 must convert to depth via AVR refactor
  / proof body started, else CHURNING")** — ADDRESSED this iter: Lane 1 IS the AVR refactor +
  proof body. The fresh `routec166` consult confirmed compliance and re-issued CONVERGING.
- **lean-vs-blueprint-checker `g0bo-iter165` 3 minor coverage gaps** (missing `\lean{...}` hints
  on `Ga`/`Gm`/`Gm.onePt`/the three ℙ¹ points/`gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero`; MulAction-prose

## Subagent verdicts (absorbed)

|---|---|---|---|
| progress-critic | routec166 | **CONVERGING** (Route C) — tripwire SATISFIED; 2-lane count CORRECT; cheapest reversal = signature/shape mismatch on Cor 1.5 V/W slots or `_hf` shape; recommends STRATEGY estimate refresh ~10–18 → ~5–12 with RR-bridge as long pole | iter-166 dispatches 2 lanes as planned.   blueprint-writer will pick up in a future hygiene iter). Re-running blueprint-reviewer this iter

## Prover lanes this iter

### Lane 1 — `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`

Refactor + proof close. Detailed objective in `PROGRESS.md`. Mathematical content lives in
`blueprint/src/chapters/AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex` (`prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant`,

L1339-1384).

### Lane 2 — `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects.lean`

Close the 7 LIVE-CONSUMER scaffold sorries (out of 9 total). Two scaffold sorries
(`ga_grpObj`, and consequently `ga_smooth`) are OFF the primary route (the 𝔾_a-additive
alternative is demoted iter-164) and may be left as `sorry` if convenient; the writer/auditor
will pick them up later. Two sub-build sorries (`projectiveLineBar_geomIrred`,
`projectiveLineBar_smoothOfRelDim`) are nice-to-have but Mathlib does not ship them for `Proj`;
flag PARTIAL if they require deep sub-builds (acceptable per the iter-165 PARTIAL gate scorecard
re-use).

## Acceptable outcomes (per-lane status target)

- **Lane 1 COMPLETE**: signature refactored to concrete `ProjectiveLineBar`; proof body closed
  modulo Lane 2's residual sorries; build green; the 3 AVR scaffold sorries (L936/L960/L989)
  are RESOLVED into actual proof bodies (with `sorryAx` propagated only through the upstream
  `Genus0BaseObjects` sorries still in flight). The headline
  `rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` still depends on the (also-deferred)
  `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` until the RR bridge closes — that's the long pole called out by

- **Lane 1 PARTIAL**: signature refactored, proof body started but instance synthesis trips on

  patch.
- **Lane 1 INCOMPLETE**: blocker phrase suggests a deeper math gap; iter-167 plan re-dispatches

- **Lane 2 PARTIAL**: ≥4 of 7 close axiom-clean (e.g. `gm_grpObj` + `gmScalingP1` body +
  `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero` body + `ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt`). The 3 ProjectiveLineBar

- **Lane 2 INCOMPLETE**: cross-domain consult required (e.g. `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` doesn't
  accept the units-functor shape for `Gm`); iter-167 lane: mathlib-analogist on the failing piece.

## End-state overview

**Zero inline `sorry`, kernel-only axioms** for Christian Merten's Jacobian
challenge. Spine = **pointed vs. unpointed** (the witness object `J` is real on
both arms; vacuity touches only the `∀ P` Albanese field for unpointed `C`). The
genus-0 rigidity content is proved over `k̄` and descended to a general base field `k`.

iter-165 landed the depth-conversion scaffold for the genus-0 base case
(`Genus0BaseObjects.lean`, 4 main objects, 3/4 with axiom-clean primary instances, 9

1. **`AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`** — **AVR refactor + proof close
